Let me continue the rebase analysis for `feat/outline-grammar`, since editor-core content there matches main's squashed version aside from doc/spec reorganization (rename of the change folder plus a few research doc additions on main that aren't on this branch).

result.output:
diff --git a/openspec/specs/document-tree-mapping/spec.md b/openspec/specs/document-tree-mapping/spec.md
index 6a741a9..3e1140d 100644
--- a/openspec/specs/document-tree-mapping/spec.md
+++ b/openspec/specs/document-tree-mapping/spec.md
@@ -1,7 +1,10 @@
 # document-tree-mapping Specification
 
 ## Purpose
-TBD - created by archiving change mapping-core. Update Purpose after archive.
+Defines the markdown ↔ block-tree mapping: how a document's headings, paragraphs, list
+items, and leaf atoms parse into a tree and encode back to markdown, with byte-identical
+round-tripping as the load-bearing guarantee every later layer (structural operations, CM6
+enforcement) depends on.
 ## Requirements
 ### Requirement: Lossless round-trip
 The library SHALL parse any markdown document into a block tree and encode any parsed tree
diff --git a/openspec/specs/structural-operations/spec.md b/openspec/specs/structural-operations/spec.md
index 3d1ec2c..3025ef6 100644
--- a/openspec/specs/structural-operations/spec.md
+++ b/openspec/specs/structural-operations/spec.md
@@ -1,7 +1,10 @@
 # structural-operations Specification
 
 ## Purpose
-TBD - created by archiving change mapping-core. Update Purpose after archive.
+Defines the structural operations (indent, outdent, moveUp, moveDown) that edit the block
+tree from document-tree-mapping: their per-node-kind algebra (heading level-shift vs.
+reparent), rejection semantics for inexpressible edits, and the closure/minimal-edit
+guarantees that keep every accepted operation's output re-parseable and diff-minimal.
 ## Requirements
 ### Requirement: Operation results are total and typed
 Every structural operation (indent, outdent, moveUp, moveDown) SHALL be a pure function

specs
design.md

---
## 1. Node split operation (mapping core)

- [ ] 1.1 Implement `splitNode(doc, nodeId, position)` in `src/ops.ts`: same-kind sibling
  split, children stay with the original, marker reuse + ordered renumber, boundary-rule
  blank for paragraphs, `cannot-split` for headings/atoms/positions outside the node's
  text
- [ ] 1.2 Unit tests for every spec scenario (mid-text, end-of-node, empty lower half,
  atom rejection, ordered-run renumber) + property tests: split results close over the
  mapping; untouched nodes keep verbatim lines; generated split positions never corrupt

## 2. Keyboard grammar (plugin)

- [ ] 2.1 Pure grammar module: binding → op resolution (node at cursor, atom-interior
  no-op rules, heading-Enter→child-paragraph rule, Shift+Enter continuation synthesis),
  returning either `null` (decline key) or a transaction plan {changes, selection,
  userEvent}; unit-tested without CM6
- [ ] 2.2 CM6 wrapper: `Prec.highest` keymap extension with per-keypress outline-mode
  check via `editorInfoField`; registered with `registerEditorExtension`; add
  `@codemirror/state`/`@codemirror/view` types (build externals unchanged)
- [ ] 2.3 Wire Tab / Shift+Tab / Alt+ArrowUp / Alt+ArrowDown to indent/outdent/move ops
  through the CM6 dispatch path (one annotated transaction, selection from op cursor,
  Notice on rejection)
- [ ] 2.4 Wire Enter (splitNode + heading rule) and Shift+Enter (continuation) with the
  same dispatch/undo semantics

## 3. Verification

- [ ] 3.1 Full gate green: tsc, lint (zero errors), vitest, plugin build
- [ ] 3.2 Extend the editor-core dev-vault protocol (verification.md) with grammar
  scenarios: off-mode stock behavior, instant toggle effect, each binding, undo

input.new_string:
## Results

Manual dev-vault run performed on the stacked branch `feat/outline-grammar` (PR open);
automated e2e coverage of the same scenarios added on `feat/e2e-harness` (PR open). See
those branches/PRs for the actual checklist results and any corpus fixtures/open-questions
updates filed from findings — not duplicated here.

## Keyboard grammar (outline-grammar change)

Scenarios below are covered by the automated e2e suite on `feat/e2e-harness`; see that
branch for actual results rather than duplicating a manual checklist here.

- Off-mode: Tab in a list, Enter, Shift+Enter, Alt+arrows all behave stock
- Toggle mode with note open: very next keypress follows the new mode
- Tab/Shift+Tab indent/outdent the node at cursor; cursor at content start
- Alt+Up/Down move nodes/sections; ordered runs renumber
- Enter mid-item splits into two items; children stay with the upper
- Enter at item end: empty `- ` sibling, cursor after marker
- Enter at paragraph end: blank line + cursor; typing creates the sibling node
- Enter on a heading: empty line below, typed text becomes a child paragraph
- Shift+Enter in an item: aligned continuation line, still one node (check by
  toggling outline off/on or via a structural op treating it as one)
- Atom interiors: Enter/Shift+Enter/Tab inside a code fence behave stock
  (whole-fence ops only from its first line)
- Each grammar op is one undo step; rejected ops change nothing but show the cue

content.text:
Rebase is complete and clean (build, `tsc`, and all 190 vitest tests pass on `feat/outline-grammar` after replaying it onto `main`). I did **not** push the rebase yet since it rewrites history — let me know if you want me to force-push.

**Archive verdict: not yet.** Here's why, from checking `openspec/changes/outline-grammar/`:

- All tasks in `tasks.md` are checked `[x]`, but task 3.2 explicitly says "pending the human run" for the dev-vault verification.
- That manual run was superseded by the automated e2e suite on `feat/e2e-harness` (as you noted) — but that branch's run **found and fixed a real bug** in `splitNode` (in [src/ops.ts](src/ops.ts)): "Enter at paragraph end" left the cursor rejoining the paragraph instead of creating a proper sibling node, which is exactly one of the scenarios outline-grammar's own `verification.md` lists as needing to hold.
- That fix (`0f9ee79` on `feat/e2e-harness`) touches `src/ops.ts` and `tests/split.test.ts` but is **not present on `feat/outline-grammar`** — it only exists on the stacked branch.

So `outline-grammar` as it stands has a known, verified-via-e2e bug in its own core deliverable (Enter/split behavior). I'd hold off archiving until that fix (or `feat/e2e-harness` itself) lands on this branch and the suite is green against it.

Want me to pull the `splitNode` fix from `feat/e2e-harness` onto `feat/outline-grammar` now, or wait until `feat/e2e-harness` merges on its own?

what's the best approach w.r.t openspec: keep this change open, merge the PR, archive it on the next branch? or merge the e2e testing branch into this one, archive and squash-merge both changes into master?

# e2e-harness — tasks

## 1. Harness scaffolding

- [x] 1.1 Add dev deps (`wdio-obsidian-service`, `@wdio/cli`,
      `@wdio/local-runner`, `@wdio/mocha-framework`, `@wdio/globals`,
      `expect-webdriverio` as needed); add `test:e2e` script that runs
      `build:plugin` first; gitignore `.obsidian-cache/`
- [x] 1.2 Create `e2e/wdio.conf.mts` (vault `test-vault`, `plugins: ["."]`,
      `browserVersion: "latest"` / `installerVersion: "earliest"`, cache dir,
      mocha framework, sane timeouts) and `e2e/tsconfig.json`; exclude `e2e/`
      from the root typecheck and confirm esbuild/vitest don't pick it up
- [x] 1.3 Write `e2e/helpers.ts`: read buffer, force-save + read disk
      bytes/mtime, read plugin `data.json`, wait-for-notice(text), set cursor
      / focus editor, platform undo chord, command-availability probe
- [x] 1.4 Smoke spec: Obsidian boots, plugin loaded, a vault note opens and
      buffer matches disk — proves the pipeline before real suites

## 2. Scenario suites

- [x] 2.1 `outline-mode.e2e.ts`: toggle by command id (notice, bytes+mtime
      unchanged), toggle via mode registry after restart (`reloadObsidian`),
      rename follows / delete prunes in `data.json`, structural commands
      gated off non-outline notes
- [x] 2.2 `structural-commands.e2e.ts`: indent/outdent paragraph round-trip
      with cursor + single-step undo, heading demote/promote with link
      resolution, skip-level outdent, moves (heading swap, ordered
      renumber), all seven rejection cues inert
- [x] 2.3 `keyboard-grammar.e2e.ts`: off-mode stock behavior, toggle applies
      to next keypress, Tab/Shift+Tab/Alt+arrows, Enter splits (mid-item,
      item end, paragraph end, heading), Shift+Enter continuation as one
      node, atom interiors stock, undo/rejection invariants per key
- [x] 2.4 `shell.e2e.ts`: clean unload removes commands; coexistence warning
      via stub conflicting plugin fixture, once-only across restart

## 3. Protocol rewrite & verification

- [x] 3.1 Rewrite `openspec/changes/editor-core/verification.md`: map each
      automated item to its e2e spec; keep manual residue (mobile smoke,
      visual cues) as a short checklist
- [x] 3.2 Full green run of `npm run test:e2e` locally; confirm `test-vault/`
      untouched (`git status`), `npm test` and `npm run build` unaffected;
      document the harness (README section: first-run download, cache, how
      to add a spec)

content.text:
Given how the two branches actually relate, I'd go with your second option — **merge `feat/e2e-harness` into `feat/outline-grammar`, archive both changes there, and land it as one PR** — not the "keep open, archive later" path. Reasoning:

- `feat/e2e-harness` isn't independent verification work — it directly completes outline-grammar's own task 3.2 ("pending the human run") and, in the process, **found and fixed a real bug in `splitNode`** that outline-grammar's own verification checklist calls out (Enter at paragraph end). Right now `feat/outline-grammar` alone has that bug; merging its PR as-is and archiving "on the next branch" means shipping a known-buggy `splitNode` to `main` and marking the change done despite the checklist explicitly saying otherwise.
- This repo's own convention (see `19c0cf2 chore: archive editor-core change, sync specs` and the equivalent mapping-core commit) is: verify → archive the change on the same branch → then merge/squash to main. Archiving before the fix lands breaks that discipline.
- `e2e-harness` and `outline-grammar` are still two distinct openspec changes/specs, so I wouldn't collapse them into one archive — just two separate `chore: archive X` commits on the combined branch, in one PR.

Concretely:
1. Rebase (or merge) `feat/e2e-harness` onto the now-rebased `feat/outline-grammar`, so the `splitNode` fix and the rewritten `verification.md` land together.
2. Run the full gate (`tsc`, lint, vitest, `test:e2e`, build).
3. Archive `outline-grammar`, then archive `e2e-harness` (sync specs each time), as separate commits.
4. Push and open one PR into `main` covering both.

Want me to do the rebase/merge of `feat/e2e-harness` onto `feat/outline-grammar` now, or do you want to review the combined diff first?

what about a third option (basically what you suggested before): cherry-pick the fix from that branch, leave the e2e automation out for now. then archive+merge this branch, then rebase+archive+merge e2e. since we squash PRs in this repo, it would be nice to keep each openspec change as a separate pr that gets squash-merged into master.
Agreed — that's the better plan, matches your PR/history convention better and still fixes the real bug before archiving. Let's do it.

fix: widen end-of-paragraph split gap so typing doesn't rejoin the sibling

Enter at paragraph end left the cursor adjacent to the paragraph text with
only one blank line, so typing there rejoined the paragraph instead of
materializing a new sibling node. Widen to two blanks and park the cursor
on the middle line, blank-separated on both sides.

Found via the e2e harness on feat/e2e-harness; cherry-picked here ahead of
that branch so this change's own verification is accurate before archiving.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
